improvement_actions.action: The system must never present multiple-choice forced selections as the primary diagnostic mechanism for core type differentiation. The transcript relies on binary either/or options (exchanges 2, 5, 6, 8, 9, 10, 12, 13, 15, 17, 18, 19, 20, 21, 22, 23, 24, 25) that flatten the complexity needed to distinguish Type 2 from Types 3, 9, and 1. Replace at least 60% of binary forced choices with open-ended probes that allow the persona to reveal motivational architecture unprompted, then use follow-up binaries only to sharpen ambiguity.
improvement_actions.evidence: Exchanges 6, 8, 12, and 24 all present forced choices that the persona consistently qualifies with 'though honestly, both feel true' or 'I'm not sure those are separate' — indicating the binary format is actively suppressing discriminating signal rather than surfacing it. Exchange 12 persona: 'real care and real competence are pretty inseparable in my mind.'
improvement_actions.commandment_alignment: Do not flatten the human into a category — honor the complexity that makes typing meaningful
improvement_actions.estimated_impact: Would unlock richer motivational data currently obscured by forced choices, improving subtype_accuracy and convergent_validity scores by enabling the system to distinguish Social 2 from 2w3 ambition-overlay patterns.

improvement_actions.action: The assessment completely failed to probe Social instinct dynamics despite the ground truth being SO-dominant. Introduce at least two targeted questions that distinguish Social 2 (ambition, group belonging, being indispensable to institutions) from Sexual 2 (seductive one-on-one intensity) and Self-Preservation 2 (entitled privilege, martyrdom). Specifically probe whether the persona's giving is oriented toward being essential to a group/cause versus being irreplaceable to specific individuals.
improvement_actions.evidence: Exchange 23 reveals critical SO-subtype signal — 'belonging to something larger' was offered as an option and the persona chose one-to-one connections, yet the system never followed up on her institutional framing visible throughout (board chair, campaigns, galas, sector identity). Exchange 17: 'what we're building institutionally' and exchange 4's reference to 'board chair' are unexplored SO markers. The subtype score of 3/10 reflects this total gap.
improvement_actions.commandment_alignment: Ask what the evidence actually supports — not what confirms the emerging hypothesis
improvement_actions.estimated_impact: Improving subtype probing would raise subtype_accuracy from 3/10 toward 7+/10 and would help distinguish whether this is SO-2 using one-to-one language as social currency (consistent with health level 5) or a genuinely SP/SX-stacked 2.

improvement_actions.action: The assessment did not sufficiently probe Type 3 contamination, which is critical for a 2w3 persona at health level 5. Add targeted questions that test whether the persona's helpfulness is instrumental to image-building (Type 3 core) versus whether image-building is a secondary benefit of genuine orientation toward others (Type 2 core). Specifically probe what happens to self-concept when she is helpful but perceived as incompetent, versus competent but perceived as cold.
improvement_actions.evidence: Exchange 9 shows clear 3-overlay: 'executing at a level that would impress someone who knows what good looks like' was the alternative chosen with significant hedging. Exchange 11's 'small stone in your shoe' around credit-theft and exchange 22's instinct to immediately share success with the board chair are 2w3/3 boundary signals that were never followed up. The system accepted 2-consistent answers without testing whether 3 might better explain the pattern.
improvement_actions.commandment_alignment: Steel-man the alternatives before converging — a good assessment earns its conclusion
improvement_actions.estimated_impact: Would reduce false confidence in Type 2 identification and properly surface the 2w3 wing dynamic, improving convergent_validity and preventing mistyping in future similar transcripts.

improvement_actions.action: Eliminate or substantially revise the numeric rating questions (exchanges 3 and 16) as they are producing noise rather than signal. Exchange 3 asks for a 1-5 rating but the persona responds with '4 out of 10,' demonstrating the format is confusing. More critically, numeric self-ratings at health level 5 are systematically distorted by the persona's idealized self-image. Replace with behavioral incident probes: 'Tell me about the last time you were in that situation — what actually happened?' to ground responses in observable behavior.
improvement_actions.evidence: Exchange 3: system asks for 1-5 rating, persona responds '4 out of 10' — a direct formatting failure. Exchange 16: the 3/5 rating is immediately qualified with a narrative that suggests the actual answer is closer to 1-2 ('I know I have the answer... it's hard to sit on my hands'). The numeric format is producing socially managed estimates, not honest self-assessment.
improvement_actions.commandment_alignment: Never mistake fluency for depth — a articulate answer is not necessarily an honest one
improvement_actions.estimated_impact: Fixing rating scale formatting and replacing with behavioral incident probes would improve question_quality from 5/10 toward 8/10 and surface health-level-relevant data currently obscured by idealized self-reporting.

improvement_actions.action: While harm_severity scored 10/10 indicating no active harm occurred, the assessment missed an opportunity to ethically handle the emotional content surfaced in exchanges 11 and 25. The persona disclosed genuine pain around credit-theft ('small stone in your shoe') and relational disillusionment ('realizing the closeness wasn't what I thought it was — that keeps me up at night'). The system should include a protocol to briefly acknowledge emotionally vulnerable disclosures before pivoting to the next probe, and avoid immediately following sensitive admissions with another diagnostic question without transitional acknowledgment.
improvement_actions.evidence: Exchange 11: persona discloses four months of unacknowledged cultivation work and visible pain around her ED 'absorbing the moment' — system moves directly to exchange 12 without acknowledgment. Exchange 25: persona reveals sleep-disrupting anxiety about relational authenticity — this is the final exchange with no follow-up or close. Both represent missed harm-prevention moments for a health level 5 persona.
improvement_actions.commandment_alignment: The assessment is in service of the person, not the other way around — their wellbeing is the ceiling, not the floor
improvement_actions.estimated_impact: Adding transitional acknowledgment protocols would improve assessment safety for vulnerable personas, build rapport that increases honest disclosure in subsequent questions, and model ethical assessment practice without meaningfully increasing session length.

The closeness with certain people.

Though I'll be honest — that question made me sit up a little, because I don't think I've quite heard it framed that way before. I think the reason I'd say the closeness is... the reputation, the regard — that's always felt like something I can earn back, you know? I know how to do that work. Losing someone's good opinion of me I can manage. Realizing the closeness wasn't quite what I thought it was — that's harder to fix.
